channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: The U.S. Economy Is Insulated From High Oil Prices. Americans Aren’t.
item.description: The overall economy has proved resilient in recent years, even as many households have struggled. The war with Iran is following the same pattern.
item.dc:creator: Ben Casselman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Debris from an intercepted Iranian drone struck an oil site in Fujairah, United Arab Emirates, on Saturday. The U.S.-led war with Iran is threatening to deliver another inflationary blow to American consumers.

item.title: A Few Ships Are Trickling Through the Strait of Hormuz With Iran’s Approval
item.description: Countries and companies have been negotiating with the Iranian authorities to secure passage. Some patterns are emerging.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Cargo ships sailing toward the Strait of Hormuz off the coast of the United Arab Emirates on Thursday.

item.title: U.S. Pauses Sanctions on Some Iranian Oil to Get More to Market
item.description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent estimated that the move would add about 140 million barrels of crude to the oil market.
item.dc:creator: Alan Rappeport
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Tierney L. Cross/The New York Times
item.media:description: Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said in a post on X that Iran would see little economic benefit from the temporary removal of the sanctions.

item.title: Striking Down Pentagon Press Limits, Judge Vindicates Independent Journalism
item.description: The ruling cut deeper than left-versus-right politics, declaring that the policy imposed by Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth is unconstitutional.
item.dc:creator: Charlie Savage
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: Reporters carried their belongings as they left the Pentagon after turning in their press passes last year.

item.title: Judge Rules Pentagon Restrictions on Press Are Unconstitutional
item.description: A federal judge tossed parts of the Pentagon’s restrictions on news outlets, saying they violated the First Amendment, in a lawsuit brought by The New York Times.
item.dc:creator: Erik Wemple
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez for The New York Times
item.media:description: A federal judge ordered the press passes of seven journalists for The New York Times to be restored.

item.title: Denmark Was Ready to Blow Up Airfields to Stop a U.S. Invasion of Greenland
item.description: The Danes brought blood supplies, explosives and live ammunition to Greenland as part of contingency plans in case President Trump acted on his threats to seize the island.
item.dc:creator: Jeffrey Gettleman, Christopher F. Schuetze and Maya Tekeli
item.category: United States Defense and Military Forces
item.media:description: According to a report in Danish public media, Nuuk Airport in Greenland was one of two airports that the Danish military was planning to disable in the event of a U.S. invasion.

item.title: Trump Friend Asked ICE to Detain the Mother of His Child
item.description: Paolo Zampolli, a former modeling agent and a longtime Trump ally, was in a custody battle over his son. An ICE official agreed to help.
item.dc:creator: Megan Twohey, Shawn McCreesh and Hamed Aleaziz

item.title: Chuck Norris, Action Star of ‘Walker, Texas Ranger,’ Dies at 86
item.description: He channeled his martial arts skills into heroic roles in films like “The Delta Force” and “Missing in Action” and in the long-running TV series “Walker, Texas Ranger.”
item.dc:creator: Clyde Haberman
item.media:description: Chuck Norris in 1995 as Cordell Walker on “Walker, Texas Ranger.” The series aired on CBS from 1993 to 2001.

item.title: Fresno Took 30 Years to Name Cesar Chavez Blvd. Undoing It Took a Day.
item.description: The reckoning for the labor leader’s sex abuse was swift in Fresno, Calif., the heart of the Central Valley region where the farmworker movement was born. Towns across the nation face similar debates.
item.dc:creator: Orlando Mayorquín

item.title: Cesar Chavez, a Civil Rights Icon, Is Accused of Abusing Girls for Years
item.description: An investigation by The New York Times found extensive evidence that the United Farm Workers co-founder groomed and sexually abused girls who worked in the movement.
item.dc:creator: Manny Fernandez and Sarah Hurtes
item.media:description: Cesar Chavez during a demonstration in New York in 1969.

item.title: Inside the Arrest That Led to Banksy’s Possible Unmasking Decades Later
item.description: In 2000, Banksy was a largely unknown street artist hanging around downtown Manhattan when the police nabbed him for trying to deface a billboard.
item.dc:creator: Corey Kilgannon
item.media:description: Banksy’s “Flower Thrower” as shown at the Banksy Museum in Lower Manhattan.

item.title: Laid Off in Midlife, China’s Reform Generation Braces for Downward Mobility
item.description: The future once seemed boundless for those who grew up during China’s reform era. Now in middle age, they are pinned between economic stagnation and institutional age discrimination.
item.dc:creator: Li Yuan

item.title: Copenhagen Grapples With the Abuse Allegations Against Noma’s Chef
item.description: René Redzepi faced swift fallout in the United States following reports of violence. But in Denmark, where he is a major cultural figure, the reaction has been more muted.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg
item.media:credit: Soeren Bidstrup/Ritzau Scanpix, via Agence France-Presse — Getty Images
item.media:description: Through his work at Noma in Copenhagen, the chef René Redzepi, right, helped elevate Denmark’s cultural reputation on the global stage.

item.title: San Francisco Driver Gets Probation After Killing a Family of 4
item.description: Mary Fong Lau, 80, will perform 200 hours of community service as part of her sentence. Her speeding vehicle crashed into a family as they waited for a bus ride to the zoo in 2024.
item.dc:creator: Heather Knight
item.media:credit: Jessica Christian/San Francisco Chronicle, via Getty Images
item.media:description: Mary Fong Lau at the Hall of Justice in San Francisco in February.

item.title: Evacuations Ordered on Oahu as Heavy Rain Brings Flash Flooding
item.description: A dam on the northern part of the island was close to failing, emergency officials said.
item.dc:creator: Aimee Ortiz, Amy Graff, Ruth Graham and Mark Walker
item.media:credit: Craig Fujii/Honolulu Civil Beat, via Associated Press
item.media:description: Floodwaters in Waialua, Hawaii, on Friday.

item.title: What Does the California Heat Mean for Fire Season?
item.description: The state is warming up and drying out earlier than usual. Here’s what that means for fire season.
item.dc:creator: Amy Graff

item.title: Nicholas Brendon, Beloved Sidekick on ‘Buffy the Vampire Slayer,’ Dies at 54
item.description: He played the part of Xander Harris, one of Buffy’s closest friends, on the hit television show about a teenage girl who protects the world from monsters.
item.dc:creator: Hannah Ziegler and Francesca Regalado
item.media:description: Nicholas Brendon, who rose to fame as the lovable sidekick on the hit show “Buffy the Vampire Slayer,” has died.

item.title: ‘The Bachelorette’ Took a Risk on Taylor Frankie Paul. It Backfired.
item.description: Ms. Paul’s built-in fan base and viral interpersonal drama presented an opportunity for the long-running reality TV show, until video of a past assault derailed the season.
item.dc:creator: Julia Jacobs and John Koblin
item.category: Mormons (Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-Day Saints)
The Secret Lives of Mormon Wives (TV Program)

item.title: Influencers Caught in ‘Bachelorette’ Fallout After ABC Pulls New Season
item.description: When ABC abruptly pulled a planned season of “The Bachelorette,” some whose livelihoods are wrapped up in the series were thrown for a loop.
item.dc:creator: Madison Malone Kircher

item.title: ‘Once and for All’ Means Never
item.description: Hamas has survived waves of killings of its leaders. More than force will be necessary to overturn the Islamic regime of Iran.
item.dc:creator: Thomas L. Friedman
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: The Future of the Democratic Party Is Emerging
item.description: On a new generation of leaders on the left.
item.dc:creator: Lis Smith and John Guida
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Citizens United v Federal Election Commission (Supreme Court Decision)
item.media:credit: Thalassa Raasch for The New York Times

item.title: ​At Least 10 Killed in South Korean Factory Fire
item.description: Survivors jumped from windows to escape the blaze, the deadliest at a factory in South Korea in two years. Four people are still missing.
item.dc:creator: Choe Sang-Hun
item.media:description: Firefighters spraying water to extinguish a fire at a factory in Daejeon, South Korea, on Friday.

item.title: 700 Dogs and Cats Are Rescued in Animal Cruelty Raid in Los Angeles County
item.description: Animal control officers responded to a pet-adoption facility in Lake Hughes, Calif., after receiving complaints about neglect and overcrowding. No charges have been filed.
item.dc:creator: Neil Vigdor

item.title: David Botstein, Gene-Mapping Pioneer, Dies at 83
item.description: His method of locating genes in human DNA allowed researchers to find disease-causing genes, and later to map the entire, sprawling human genome.
item.dc:creator: Gina Kolata

item.title: ‘Africa Will Write Its Own History.’ Who Was Patrice Lumumba?
item.description: Remembering the African leader after a Brussels court this week ordered a retired Belgian diplomat stand trial for his 1961 assassination.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Mpoke Bigg
item.category: Coups D'Etat and Attempted Coups D'Etat
War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
item.media:description: Patrice Lumumba in New York in July 1960. He helped liberate Congo from brutal Belgian rule in 1960 and became the nation’s first prime minister after independence.
